metadata.title: High School — Geometry
metadata.notes: Auto-ingested from Common Standards Project (set 67810E9EF6944F9383DCC602A3484C23_D10003FB_high-school-geometry). Node structure matches KnowledgeNode in knowledge_zome/integrity/src/lib.rs.

nodes.title: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e
nodes.description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e.g., designing an object or structure to satisfy physical constraints or minimize cost; working with typographic grid systems based on ratios).

academic_standards.description: Apply geometric methods to solve design problems (e.g., designing an object or structure to satisfy physical constraints or minimize cost; working with typographic grid systems based on ratios).

nodes.title: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e
nodes.description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e.g., persons per square mile, BTUs per cubic foot).

academic_standards.description: Apply concepts of density based on area and volume in modeling situations (e.g., persons per square mile, BTUs per cubic foot).

nodes.title: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe object...
nodes.description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects (e.g., modeling a tree trunk or a human torso as a cylinder).

academic_standards.description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe objects (e.g., modeling a tree trunk or a human torso as a cylinder).

nodes.title: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional ob...
nodes.description: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects, and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional objects.

academic_standards.description: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects, and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional objects.

nodes.title: Use volume formulas for cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve prob...
nodes.description: Use volume formulas for cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.

academic_standards.description: Use volume formulas for cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems.

nodes.title: (+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas fo...
nodes.description: (+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures.

academic_standards.description: (+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures.

nodes.title: Give an informal argument for the formulas for the circumference of a circle,...
nodes.description: Give an informal argument for the formulas for the circumference of a circle, area of a circle, volume of a cylinder, pyramid, and cone.

academic_standards.description: Give an informal argument for the formulas for the circumference of a circle, area of a circle, volume of a cylinder, pyramid, and cone.

nodes.title: Use coordinates to compute perimeters of polygons and areas of triangles and ...
nodes.description: Use coordinates to compute perimeters of polygons and areas of triangles and rectangles, e.g., using the distance formula.

academic_standards.description: Use coordinates to compute perimeters of polygons and areas of triangles and rectangles, e.g., using the distance formula.

nodes.title: Find the point on a directed line segment between two given points that parti...
nodes.description: Find the point on a directed line segment between two given points that partitions the segment in a given ratio.

academic_standards.description: Find the point on a directed line segment between two given points that partitions the segment in a given ratio.

nodes.title: Prove the slope criteria for parallel and perpendicular lines and use them to...
nodes.description: Prove the slope criteria for parallel and perpendicular lines and use them to solve geometric problems (e.g., find the equation of a line parallel or perpendicular to a given line that passes through a given point).

academic_standards.description: Prove the slope criteria for parallel and perpendicular lines and use them to solve geometric problems (e.g., find the equation of a line parallel or perpendicular to a given line that passes through a given point).

nodes.description: Use coordinates to prove simple geometric theorems algebraically.

nodes.title: (+) Derive the equations of ellipses and hyperbolas given the foci, using the...
nodes.description: (+) Derive the equations of ellipses and hyperbolas given the foci, using the fact that the sum or difference of distances from the foci is constant.

nodes.description: Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.

academic_standards.description: Derive the equation of a circle of given center and radius using the Pythagorean Theorem; complete the square to find the center and radius of a circle given by an equation.

nodes.title: Derive using similarity the fact that the length of the arc intercepted by an...
nodes.description: Derive using similarity the fact that the length of the arc intercepted by an angle is proportional to the radius, and define the radian measure of the angle as the constant of proportionality; derive the formula for the area of a sector.

academic_standards.description: Derive using similarity the fact that the length of the arc intercepted by an angle is proportional to the radius, and define the radian measure of the angle as the constant of proportionality; derive the formula for the area of a sector.

nodes.title: Use trigonometric ratios and the Pythagorean Theorem to solve right triangles...

nodes.title: Understand that by similarity, side ratios in right triangles are properties ...
nodes.description: Understand that by similarity, side ratios in right triangles are properties of the angles in the triangle, leading to definitions of trigonometric ratios for acute angles.

academic_standards.description: Understand that by similarity, side ratios in right triangles are properties of the angles in the triangle, leading to definitions of trigonometric ratios for acute angles.

nodes.title: Given two figures, use the definition of similarity in terms of similarity tr...
nodes.description: Given two figures, use the definition of similarity in terms of similarity transformations to decide if they are similar; explain using similarity transformations the meaning of similarity for triangles as the equality of all corresponding pairs of angles and the proportionality of all corresponding pairs of sides.

academic_standards.description: Given two figures, use the definition of similarity in terms of similarity transformations to decide if they are similar; explain using similarity transformations the meaning of similarity for triangles as the equality of all corresponding pairs of angles and the proportionality of all corresponding pairs of sides.

nodes.title: Construct an equilateral triangle, a square, and a regular hexagon inscribed ...

nodes.title: Make formal geometric constructions with a variety of tools and methods (comp...
nodes.description: Make formal geometric constructions with a variety of tools and methods (compass and straightedge, string, reflective devices, paper folding, dynamic geometric software, etc.). Copying a segment; copying an angle; bisecting a segment; bisecting an angle; constructing perpendicular lines, including the perpendicular bisector of a line segment; and constructing a line parallel to a given line through a point not on the line.

academic_standards.description: Make formal geometric constructions with a variety of tools and methods (compass and straightedge, string, reflective devices, paper folding, dynamic geometric software, etc.). Copying a segment; copying an angle; bisecting a segment; bisecting an angle; constructing perpendicular lines, including the perpendicular bisector of a line segment; and constructing a line parallel to a given line through a point not on the line.

nodes.title: Explain how the criteria for triangle congruence (ASA, SAS, and SSS) follow f...
nodes.description: Explain how the criteria for triangle congruence (ASA, SAS, and SSS) follow from the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions.

nodes.title: Use the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions to show that two t...
nodes.description: Use the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions to show that two triangles are congruent if and only if corresponding pairs of sides and corresponding pairs of angles are congruent.

academic_standards.description: Use the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions to show that two triangles are congruent if and only if corresponding pairs of sides and corresponding pairs of angles are congruent.

nodes.title: Use geometric descriptions of rigid motions to transform figures and to predi...
nodes.description: Use geometric descriptions of rigid motions to transform figures and to predict the effect of a given rigid motion on a given figure; given two figures, use the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions to decide if they are congruent.

academic_standards.description: Use geometric descriptions of rigid motions to transform figures and to predict the effect of a given rigid motion on a given figure; given two figures, use the definition of congruence in terms of rigid motions to decide if they are congruent.

nodes.title: Given a geometric figure and a rotation, reflection, or translation, draw the...
nodes.description: Given a geometric figure and a rotation, reflection, or translation, draw the transformed figure using, e.g., graph paper, tracing paper, or geometry software. Specify a sequence of transformations that will carry a given figure onto another.

academic_standards.description: Given a geometric figure and a rotation, reflection, or translation, draw the transformed figure using, e.g., graph paper, tracing paper, or geometry software. Specify a sequence of transformations that will carry a given figure onto another.

nodes.title: Develop definitions of rotations, reflections, and translations in terms of a...
nodes.description: Develop definitions of rotations, reflections, and translations in terms of angles, circles, perpendicular lines, parallel lines, and line segments.

academic_standards.description: Develop definitions of rotations, reflections, and translations in terms of angles, circles, perpendicular lines, parallel lines, and line segments.

nodes.title: Given a rectangle, parallelogram, trapezoid, or regular polygon, describe the...
nodes.description: Given a rectangle, parallelogram, trapezoid, or regular polygon, describe the rotations and reflections that carry it onto itself.

academic_standards.description: Given a rectangle, parallelogram, trapezoid, or regular polygon, describe the rotations and reflections that carry it onto itself.

nodes.title: Represent transformations in the plane using, e
nodes.description: Represent transformations in the plane using, e.g., transparencies and geometry software; describe transformations as functions that take points in the plane as inputs and give other points as outputs. Compare transformations that preserve distance and angle to those that do not (e.g., translation versus horizontal stretch).

academic_standards.description: Represent transformations in the plane using, e.g., transparencies and geometry software; describe transformations as functions that take points in the plane as inputs and give other points as outputs. Compare transformations that preserve distance and angle to those that do not (e.g., translation versus horizontal stretch).

nodes.title: Know precise definitions of angle, circle, perpendicular line, parallel line,...
nodes.description: Know precise definitions of angle, circle, perpendicular line, parallel line, and line segment, based on the undefined notions of point, line, distance along a line, and distance around a circular arc.

academic_standards.description: Know precise definitions of angle, circle, perpendicular line, parallel line, and line segment, based on the undefined notions of point, line, distance along a line, and distance around a circular arc.
